heading: Regulations
label: 7505 Regulations
notes: Amendments2019—Subsec. (d). added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 116–1031997—Subsec. (c). substituted “small business concerns, qualified HUBZone small business concerns, and” for “small business concerns and”.Pub. L. 105–1351996— reenacted section catchline without change and amended text generally, substituting present provisions for similar provisions relating to regulations, including implementation guidelines for regulations, criteria for determining appropriate charges to programs of Federal financial assistance for cost of audits, and guidelines to ensure that small business concerns and business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals have opportunity to participate in contracts awarded to fulfill audit requirements of this chapter.Pub. L. 104–156
Effective Date of 1997 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under , Commerce and Trade.Pub. L. 105–135section 3 of Pub. L. 105–135section 631 of Title 15Oct. 1, 1997

Guidance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–103, § 5(b)133 Stat. 3270Dec. 30, 2019“Not later than 3 years after the date of enactment of this Act [], the Director [of the Office of Management and Budget] shall issue guidance requiring audit-related information reported under chapter 75 of title 31, United States Code, to be reported in an electronic form consistent with the data standards established under chapter 64 of that title, as added by section 4(a) of this Act.”Dec. 30, 2019

Guidelines for Acceptance of Audits by State and Local Governments Receiving Federal Assistance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–201, div. A, title VIII, § 808(c)110 Stat. 2607Sept. 23, 1996“The Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall issue guidelines to ensure that an audit of indirect costs performed by the Federal Government is accepted by State and local governments that receive Federal funds under contracts, grants, or other Federal assistance programs.”

Guidelines for Acceptance of Audits by State and Local Governments Receiving Federal Assistance, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–201, div. A, title VIII, § 808(c)110 Stat. 2607Sept. 23, 1996“The Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall issue guidelines to ensure that an audit of indirect costs performed by the Federal Government is accepted by State and local governments that receive Federal funds under contracts, grants, or other Federal assistance programs.”

children.label: a
children.text: The Director, after consultation with the Comptroller General, and appropriate officials from Federal, State, and local governments and nonprofit organizations shall prescribe guidance to implement this chapter. Each Federal agency shall promulgate such amendments to its regulations as may be necessary to conform such regulations to the requirements of this chapter and of such guidance.

children.label: i
children.text: not conducted in accordance with this chapter; or

children.label: ii
children.text: conducted in accordance with this chapter when expenditures of Federal awards are less than amounts cited in section 7502(a)(1)(A) or specified by the Director under section 7502(a)(3), except that the Director may allow the cost of limited scope audits to monitor subrecipients in accordance with section 7502(f)(2)(B); and

children.label: B
children.text: more than a reasonably proportionate share of the cost of any such audit that is conducted in accordance with this chapter.

The criteria prescribed pursuant to paragraph (1) shall not, in the absence of documentation demonstrating a higher actual cost, permit the percentage of the cost of audits performed pursuant to this chapter charged to Federal awards, to exceed the ratio of total Federal awards expended by such non-Federal entity during the applicable fiscal year or years, to such non-Federal entity’s total expenditures during such fiscal year or years.

children.label: c
children.text: Such guidance shall include such provisions as may be necessary to ensure that small business concerns, qualified HUBZone small business concerns, and business concerns owned and controlled by socially and economically disadvantaged individuals will have the opportunity to participate in the performance of contracts awarded to fulfill the audit requirements of this chapter.

children.label: d
children.text: Such guidance shall require audit-related information reported under this chapter to be reported in an electronic form in accordance with the data standards established under chapter 64.
